releases.description:
## Bug Fix

Surfaces `isArchived` from `MemoryService.getStates` for tagged states. The SQLite-metadata fast-path skipped the JSONL content fetch when `stateMeta.tags` was cached, and the skeleton return path never surfaced `isArchived` to consumers. As a result, archiving a tagged state in the workspace-settings UI did not remove it from the list, and the AI-facing `listStates` tool returned archived tagged states as if active (partially breaking the AI-archive-only read contract introduced in v5.9.6 / #215).

Surgical 1-line fix: drop the `stateMeta.tags ? null :` shortcut at `MemoryService.ts:555`. Tool filter at `listStates.ts:106` inherits the fix automatically. Adds 3 regression tests covering tagged-archived surfacing, tags preservation, and explicit no-shortcut assertion.

### What this affects
- **UI** — Workspace settings → States section: archive icon now correctly removes tagged states from the list.
- **AI** — `listStates` MCP tool now correctly respects the archive flag on tagged states.

### Follow-up tech debt (not in this release)
Proper long-term fix is denormalizing `is_archived` into SQLite metadata so the fast-path can return a complete view (~80-120 LoC, v13 migration). The shortcut existed for perf — denormalization restores it without correctness cost. Tracked as a separate issue.

**PR**: #218 (follow-up to #216 / #215 manual-test finding)

## What's Changed
* feat: state CRUA tools + states management UI (#215) by @ProfSynapse in https://github.com/ProfSynapse/nexus/pull/216
* fix(memoryManager): surface isArchived from getStates for tagged states by @ProfSynapse in https://github.com/ProfSynapse/nexus/pull/218


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/ProfSynapse/nexus/compare/5.9.6...v5.9.7

releases.author: ProfSynapse
releases.description:
## What's New

### Pattern-anchored \`content replace\` (PR #206) — breaking schema change

**Old shape** (≤ v5.8.x): \`{path, oldContent, newContent, startLine, endLine}\` — line-number anchored.
**New shape** (v5.9.0+): \`{path, start, end, content}\` — 4-field, pattern-anchored. Both \`ContentManager.replace\` and \`executePrompts.replace\` migrated in lockstep. No compat shim — the old shape returns a clean validation error.

\`start\` and \`end\` are independent globally-unique anchor strings. The tool deletes the byte range from the start of the line containing \`start\` through the end of the line containing \`end\`, then inserts \`content\`. Whole-line semantics absorb the matched lines so callers don't need to micro-target byte offsets. NFKC + CRLF normalization applies to the compare path only — file bytes outside the replacement range are written verbatim.

**Why this design**: LLM clients have weaker priors about file position than file content. A line-number anchor that \"mostly works\" silently latches onto the wrong line when the file shifts; an anchor string that \"mostly works\" fails *loudly* — emitting an ambiguity error with the actual matching line numbers, which coaches the LLM to extend the anchor on retry. Globally-unique anchors (vs sed-style \"first end after start\") eliminate the silent latch-onto-wrong-end failure mode at the cost of one additional uniqueness check.

**Migration**: any caller passing the old 5-field shape gets a validation error. Update to the 4-field shape; the new error messages name the exact violation (anchor not found / ambiguous with line list / whitespace-only / order reversed / empty content).

46/46 jest green, \`tsc --noEmit\` clean. Reviewer verdicts: architect APPROVED, test GREEN, backend GREEN.

## Install

Download \`main.js\`, \`connector.js\`, \`manifest.json\`, and \`styles.css\` into your vault's \`.obsidian/plugins/nexus/\` folder, then reload the plugin.

releases.author: ProfSynapse
releases.description:
Hotfix for v5.8.12 (cloud-sync cache backend) regression discovered post-deploy on Windows.

## What was wrong

After v5.8.12 shipped, Windows users with an existing cache.db saw the migration run on every Obsidian restart with the same log signature each time:
```
[CacheBackendMigration] READ_LEGACY ok
[CacheBackendMigration] WRITE_IDB ok
[CacheBackendMigration] VERIFY ok
[CacheBackendMigration] MARK_VERIFIED ok
[CacheBackendMigration] JANITOR done (removed=0, failed=1)
```

## Root cause

`PluginScopedStorageCoordinator.saveState` was unconditionally replacing `pluginData.pluginStorage` on every boot during storage-plan preparation, wiping the `cacheBackend` field that the migration FSM had just persisted. On the next boot the migration saw no persisted verified state and ran the full FSM again.

On macOS, JANITOR successfully deletes cache.db on the fire-and-forget pass after MARK_VERIFIED, so the next boot's legacyExists check returns false and the migration short-circuits via the fresh-install fast-path — the saveState clobber bug is invisible. On Windows, the Obsidian filesystem watcher holds a transient handle on cache.db and JANITOR's single-shot adapter.remove fails — legacyExists stays true and the full FSM re-runs every boot.

## What changed

1. `PluginScopedStorageCoordinator.saveState` now merges existing `cacheBackend` into the new state before saveData.
2. `CacheBackendMigration.runJanitor` now retries the literal cache.db delete with 100/250/500ms backoff (3 attempts, 850ms total budget). Per spec §5.4 JANITOR is still fire-and-forget — failure never blocks startup.
3. New regression tests pin the saveState preservation contract; existing JANITOR retry test rewritten.

## Quality

- 99/99 cache-backend tests green
- Build clean
- 3 new regression tests in PluginScopedStorageCoordinator.cacheBackendPreservation.test.ts

PR: #203
releases.name: v5.8.13 — Cache backend re-run loop hotfix

releases.author: ProfSynapse
releases.description:
Patch release for PR #202.

## What's fixed
Resolves the GDrive Shared Drive boot-hang incident where the SQLite cache file (`cache.db`) was being conflict-copied by cloud-sync clients, corrupting handle state and producing indefinite hangs in the Task Board ("waiting for services") and MCP `getTools`/`useTools` calls.

## What changed
- **Storage backend swap on desktop**: file-based SQLite cache replaced with IndexedDB via new `CacheBlobStore` interface seam. Desktop now uses `IndexedDBCacheBlobStore`; mobile keeps `VaultAdapterCacheBlobStore`. SQLite itself is untouched — the in-memory `sqlite3_deserialize` path is preserved; only the persistence backend changes.
- **One-time migration on first boot**: every existing desktop install with an on-disk `cache.db` triggers a foreground-blocking migration FSM (DETECT → READ_LEGACY → WRITE_IDB → VERIFY → MARK_VERIFIED → JANITOR → DONE). Migration is idempotent and verified; legacy `cache.db` and any conflict-copy siblings (`cache (N).db`, `cache N.db`, `cache (Conflict).db`) are janitored after VERIFY.
- **Sticky restart Notice on first migration boot**: papers over a transient `waitForQueryReady` race during post-migration reconciliation. Restart skips the bad path permanently. Root-cause investigation tracked as a follow-up.
- **New command**: `Nexus: Rebuild cache` — re-entrancy-guarded `rebuildCache()` for manual cache rebuild from JSONL source-of-truth.

## Mobile
Unchanged. `isDesktopOnly: false` preserved. Mobile keeps the existing `vault.adapter` file backend via `VaultAdapterCacheBlobStore`.

## Quality
- 99/99 cache-backend tests green (53 new tests across 6 unit + 7 integration files, on top of 32 smoke tests)
- Full suite: 2724 passed, 1 pre-existing unrelated failure (ModelAgentManager, predates this PR)
- 0 Blocking findings; all 7 Minor remediated pre-merge (3 code fixes + 7 test additions)
- Runtime-verified on Synaptic Labs vault (Google Drive Shared Drive) — original conflict-copy hang resolved on second boot

## Known follow-ups
- `CONFLICT_COPY_PATTERNS` regex doesn't match Dropbox's canonical `cache (User's conflicted copy YYYY-MM-DD).db` form — tracked for post-merge regex widening
- First-boot transient `waitForQueryReady` race during post-migration reconciliation — papered over with the restart Notice; root-cause investigation deferred

## Artifacts
`main.js`, `connector.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`

Bare CSV (`[[A]],[[B]]`) is left untouched, and the documented JSON-array workaround (`["[[A]]","[[B]]"]`) still works. 170/170 `ToolManagerCliSyntax` tests pass with three new repro tests covering the triple-bracket wikilink case on both code paths and the bare-CSV counter-case. releases.author: ProfSynapse
releases.description:
## What's Fixed

- **Sync-safe storage reconcile (Phase 1)** — fixes the GDrive Shared Drive multi-device task-revert incident where collaborator edits silently reverted on sync. Three layers ship together:
  1. **Conflict-copy regex relaxation** in the shard reader and JSONL watcher so `[Conflict]`, `(N)`, `_conf(N)`, Dropbox-style and iCloud sibling files are no longer dropped silently.
  2. **New `ReconcilePipeline`** with three-layer idempotency (cursor `lastEventId` tail-match fast-path + `applied_events` PK dedupe + INSERT OR REPLACE) — handles the silent-overwrite mode (full-file replacement, no conflict sibling) that was the actual incident pattern.
  3. **v11→v12 additive `shard_cursors` migration** — per-file cursor keyed by full shardPath (canonical and conflict-sibling shards never collapsed).

- **Wire-up**: `HybridStorageAdapter.handleExternalJsonlChange` now invokes scoped per-shard reconcile on external mtime change (additive — full-rebuild path preserved). Manual `Nexus: Refresh synced data` Notice now reads `Reconciliation complete.`

- **Test coverage**: 73 new Jest tests green including the architect §6 silent-overwrite green-bar that reproduces the user incident; perf gate 13ms warm @ 10K events.

- **Mobile**: `isDesktopOnly: false` preserved — static AST sweep enforces no top-level Node.js built-in imports across new code.

Phase 2 (cache.db relocation outside the vault, byte-offset cursor) deferred — this release ships on the three-layer idempotency that was load-bearing for the actual incident pattern.

## Install

Download `main.js`, `connector.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/nexus/` folder.

releases.author: ProfSynapse
releases.description:
## Highlights

**Workspace/memory/search batch + hydration race fix.** Six PRs since 5.8.7. The headline fix is **#197** (issue #190): `loadWorkspace` no longer returns `Workspace not found` and `listStates` no longer silently returns `[]` when called immediately after `Cmd+P → Reload`. Thanks to @gcp007-ops for the empirical hydration-race diagnosis with `withReadableBackend` telemetry.

## Bug fixes

- **#197 / #190** — `HybridStorageAdapter.waitForQueryReady` is now event-based (settled by the existing `complete`/`clear`/`fail` phase transitions) instead of polling every 250ms. `withReadableBackend` awaits `waitForQueryReady()` once before falling through to the legacy backend, closing the 1–3s warm hydration window (30–60s on cold boot) where reads silently hit a stale legacy view. The 60s timeout is demoted to a safety net for stuck hydration only.

## Workspace + session

- **#192** — Threads `workspaceId` through the session handle pipeline. `sessionHandleMap` is now partitioned per workspace and evicted on session delete, so sessions no longer leak across workspace boundaries. Adds `moveSessionToWorkspace` for explicit reassignment.
- **#193** — `WorkspaceFolderWatcher` observes vault folder changes for the active workspace. `WorkspaceContextBuilder` is expanded to assemble richer context for `loadWorkspace`. `recordActivityTrace` is deduped against the explicit `getSession` path.
- **#195** — `displaySessionId` and `sessionName` are plumbed through the tool batch executor, request handlers, and connector. The internal corrected UUID stays internal (`correctedId === originalSessionId`); only the human-readable label crosses the wire.

## Search + memory

- **#194** — `MemorySearchProcessor` gains CLI-trace pretty-printing and `useTools` result expansion (`expandUseToolsTraceMatches`, `formatUseToolsResultContent`, `parseCliSegmentArgs`, etc.). The file is now 914 LoC; tracked as Phase 2 extraction candidate (split into `TraceMatchExpander` + `UseToolsResultFormatter` when next functionally touched).

## Eval harness

- **#196** — Eval harness in `tests/eval/` gains retry-on-transient-error, structured YAML config, broader scenario coverage, and a `/nexus-eval-harness` skill for reproducible invocation.

## Install

Download `main.js`, `connector.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/nexus/` folder.

releases.author: ProfSynapse
releases.description:
## Fixes

- **CLI parser — comma separator is whitespace-gated** (PR #181, fixes carry-over from #163): `splitTopLevelSegments` no longer treats every unquoted comma as a command separator. A comma is structural only when followed by whitespace/EOF (canonical `cmd1, cmd2` form). Glued commas (`a,b,c`) stay textual, so CSV array flag values like `--paths a,b,c` no longer explode into pseudo-commands (`Unknown agent "b"`). 9 regression tests added.

- **CLI parser — phantom backslash dropped for non-canonical escapes** (PR #180, fixes #179): `unescapeQuotedContent` default branch was re-emitting the backslash for any `\X` outside the canonical set (`\n \r \t \" \' \\ \uXXXX`), silently corrupting backticks, fenced code blocks, `$`, `#`, `(`, `)` and any shell-ish character an LLM might defensively escape. Now aligns with POSIX-shell double-quoted semantics: `\X` with no special meaning becomes `X`. Literal `\X` is still reachable via `\\X`. 11 regression cases added.

- **Task Board auto-refresh on storage external-sync** (PR #176): the Task Board view now subscribes to storage external-sync events and routes refreshes through the existing `TaskBoardSyncCoordinator` so open boards reload when task streams change. Note-link mutations also emit board update notifications so card metadata stays live without reopening the view.

Download `main.js`, `connector.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/nexus/` folder.

releases.author: ProfSynapse
releases.description:
## What's New

- **Replace tool**: New `contentManager.replace` tool validates `oldContent` at specified lines before writing. On mismatch, searches the full note and reports where the content actually is (single match, multiple matches, or not found). Handles both replace and delete (`newContent=""`) operations.
- **Insert tool**: New `contentManager.insert` tool adds content at a position (`startLine=N`), prepends (`startLine=1`), or appends (`startLine=-1`). No overwrite validation needed.
- **Removed**: Unified `update` tool replaced by the above two focused tools.

## Why

The old `update` tool combined 5 modes (insert, replace, delete, append, prepend) into one tool with complex mode detection. Splitting into `replace` and `insert` gives LLMs a clear decision: "Am I changing existing content or adding new content?" The replace tool's `oldContent` validation prevents silent overwrites when line numbers shift between read and write operations.

Inspired by community feedback in #56.

## Install

Download `main.js`, `connector.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/nexus/` folder.

releases.author: ProfSynapse

## Bug Fixes

### IPC Socket Lifecycle Fix
Previously, when a connector process died unexpectedly, the MCP transport slot was never released. This left `Protocol._transport` permanently set, causing all subsequent reconnection attempts to be silently rejected with "Already connected" while file descriptors continued to leak.

The fix wires the raw socket's `close` and `end` events to `transport.close()`, so the SDK's Protocol resets properly when a client disconnects. Sockets are also destroyed on failed `connect()` calls to prevent FD accumulation.

This means Claude Code, Claude Desktop, and other MCP clients now reconnect cleanly after a crash or restart without requiring an Obsidian plugin reload.

**Credit**: Thanks to [@DylanLacey](https://github.com/DylanLacey) for identifying and fixing this bug.
